_comment: Who this Cella instance belongs to. Copy to roster.json and point CELLA_ROSTER at it.
A Constitutional Committee seat may be held by a consortium of delegates or by a
single member — both are common. Cella does not assume the first: list one member
and the chamber describes itself in the singular.
name / short  the body's name, and a short form for headings ('Cardano Curia' / 'The Curia').
kind          what the body is, e.g. 'Constitutional Committee member'.
logo          a path Cella SERVES — not an external URL. The Content-Security-Policy is
img-src 'self', deliberately, and a governance tool should not go dark
because someone else's server did. Point CELLA_LOGO at a file on disk and
Cella serves it at /brand/logo; set this to '/brand/logo' to use it.
For each member:
handle      how they are known publicly (an X handle). Shown on the dashboard, so the
body reads as a group of people rather than a list of strings.
address     their Cardano wallet address (bech32). This is how Cella recognises them at
sign-in: the public key inside their wallet signature must hash to a
credential this address carries. A member with no address cannot sign in.
voteKeyHash their on-chain voting credential (hex, blake2b-224) — the key hash that
appears in the hot NFT datum's voting group and that signs the committee's
vote transaction. Usually NOT the same key as the wallet: the wallet gets a
member into Cella, the voting key gets the committee's vote onto the chain.
Leave it empty until you have it.
name: Cardano Curia
blurb: A consortium that deliberates on Cardano governance actions, assesses their constitutionality, and casts a single committee vote with a shared rationale.
